message:
fix(lab/oracle): detect deadline violations when child regions arrive before their parent

The DeadlineMonotoneOracle only checked the monotonicity invariant
(child deadline <= parent deadline) when the parent was already
registered.  In deterministic lab runs, region creation events can
arrive out of order — a child region may be registered before its
parent.  When the parent later arrived with a tighter deadline,
the existing violation against the child was never detected.

Fix: Extract record_violation_if_needed() helper to deduplicate
violation recording.  New check_existing_children() method scans
for already-registered children when a new region is created,
catching the reversed-arrival case.  on_region_create() now calls
check_existing_children() after inserting the region.

Tests:
- child_created_before_parent_violation_detected_when_parent_arrives:
  child (deadline=1000) registered before parent (deadline=500),
  violation detected when parent is inserted.
- grandchild_created_before_intermediate_parent_violation_detected_on_parent_insert:
  three-level hierarchy with grandchild and grandparent registered
  first, intermediate parent triggers violation detection on insert.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
